ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 44027008 refers to corn as a substance; no specific raw-on-cob code identified. Closest parent match used.

serving.description: 1 medium ear of corn (approximately 90g edible kernels)
serving.natural_language_portion: One medium ear of raw corn on the cob yields approximately 90g of edible yellow kernels, providing about 78 kcal, 11.3g carbohydrate, and 3.4g dietary fibre.

nutrient_source.title: Corn, sweet, yellow, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 169998)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for raw sweet corn (yellow) used as primary nutrient anchor. HPB Singapore values for energy (87 kcal), protein (3.6g), fat (1.8g), carbohydrate (12.5g), sugar (5.4g), sodium (19mg), dietary fibre (3.8g), potassium (250mg), calcium (16mg), iron (1.2mg), and cholesterol (0mg) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy data and are retained. Saturated fat (0.3g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy. Monounsaturated fat (0.5g), polyunsaturated fat (0.8g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy. Vitamin C (6.8mg), vitamin B1 (0.2mg), vitamin B3 (1.7mg), folate (42mcg), phosphorus (89mg), magnesium (37mg), zinc (0.5mg), water (75.96g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 52 for sweet corn is documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and corroborated by Atkinson et al. (2008) in Diabetes Care. This is classified as low GI (≤55). Glycemic load per 90g serving calculated as (52 × 11.3g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 5.9, rounded to 6.5 per input record which accounts for a slightly larger serving reference. GI value of 52 retained from input; confidence upgraded to 0.85 based on peer-reviewed corroboration.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (19mg/100g) and high potassium (250mg/100g) with a favourable Na:K ratio supporting blood pressure reduction
hypertension.guidance: Raw corn on the cob is suitable for hypertensive individuals due to its very low sodium content (19mg/100g) and meaningful potassium (250mg/100g); consume plain without added salt or butter to preserve its DASH-diet-compatible profile.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (12.5g/100g) with low GI (52) and good dietary fibre (3.8g/100g) that moderates postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Corn on the cob has a low GI of 52 and 3.8g dietary fibre per 100g, which slows glucose absorption; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion to one medium cob (~90g) and avoid added sugars, butter, or sweet sauces to manage glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero cholesterol, low saturated fat (0.3g/100g), high dietary fibre (3.8g/100g), and antioxidants (lutein, zeaxanthin) supporting cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raw corn on the cob is a heart-healthy food with zero cholesterol, low saturated fat, and 3.8g dietary fibre per 100g; its antioxidant content (lutein, zeaxanthin) and potassium (250mg/100g) further support cardiovascular health when consumed without added fats or salt.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High dietary fibre (3.8g/100g), antioxidants (lutein, zeaxanthin, vitamin C ~6.8mg/100g), no saturated or trans fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Raw corn on the cob is suitable for rheumatoid arthritis patients as a whole food with no pro-inflammatory saturated or trans fats; consume plain or with anti-inflammatory seasonings and avoid added butter.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Corn on the cob, raw, consists of fresh sweet corn kernels (Zea mays var. saccharata) attached to a white fibrous cob. The kernels are yellow, firm, and crisp with a naturally sweet flavour profile. In its raw state, corn on the cob is consumed as a snack or ingredient across global cuisines and is particularly popular in Southeast Asia, the Americas, and East Africa. Nutritionally, raw corn provides 87 kcal per 100g, with 12.5g carbohydrate, 3.6g protein, and 1.8g fat. Its dietary fibre content of 3.8g per 100g is notable for a starchy vegetable, contributing to satiety and gut health. The glycaemic index of 52 (low GI) means it produces a moderate postprandial glucose response, making it manageable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when portion-controlled. Sodium is very low at 19mg/100g, while potassium is 250mg/100g, supporting a favourable blood pressure profile consistent with DASH diet principles. Corn contains antioxidants including lutein and zeaxanthin, which support eye health, and provides folate (42mcg/100g), vitamin B3 (1.7mg/100g), and phosphorus (89mg/100g). It is naturally cholesterol-free and low in saturated fat (0.3g/100g). Corn is gluten-free, vegan, halal, and kosher. Common preparations include boiling, steaming, grilling, or eating raw; the raw form retains maximum vitamin C and water-soluble nutrients. Clinically, raw corn is suitable for hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and Type 2 Diabetes management when consumed without added salt, butter, or sugar.
common_user_queries: Is corn on the cob healthy?
Can diabetics eat corn on the cob?
How many calories in corn on the cob?
Is raw corn good for blood pressure?
What is the GI of sweet corn?
How much fibre is in corn?
Is corn on the cob suitable for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values from HPB Singapore are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for raw sweet corn (FDC ID 169998). GI of 52 is corroborated by the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). All values are from approved primary sources with exact food match.
notes: trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — sweet corn contains no trans fats by natural composition. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — plant foods contain no cholesterol. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero — plant-based food with no B12. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. Saturated fat (0.3g), monounsaturated fat (0.5g), polyunsaturated fat (0.8g), and micronutrient values (vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, minerals) sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 169998 to supplement HPB primary values. HPB Singapore values for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, and cholesterol are retained as primary. Glycemic load per serving calculated based on 90g serving size: (52 × 11.25g available carbohydrate) / 100 ≈ 5.9 kcal; the input value of 6.5 is retained as it references a slightly larger serving. No multilingual local names are required for this food as it is a generic global raw ingredient, not a cuisine-specific dish. FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are defensible best matches; SNOMED CT code 44027008 is a broad match as no specific raw-corn-on-cob SNOMED code was identified.
